title: ST_558_Project1
text: This project aims to use the U.S. Census API to extract and analyze individualized data from the U.S. Census PUMS dataset. The goal is to build a flexible data processing workflow that enables researches to query, process, and analyze survey data using weighted statistical methods. Due to the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic, data from 2020 has been excluded, providing an opportunity to compare data patterns before and after the pandemic.

title: ST_558_Project1
text: This project aims to use the U.S. Census API to extract and analyze individualized data from the U.S. Census PUMS dataset. title: ST_558_Project1
ST_558_Project1
section: Writing a Generic Function for Summarizing
text:
Writing a Generic Function for Summarizing

# ---- Summary Function ----
summary.census <- function(object, num_var , cat_var ){
  x <- object[[num_var]]
  w <- object$PWGTP
  
  w_mean <- sum(x*w)/sum(w)
  cat_counts <- table(object[[cat_var]])
  
  list(
    weighted_mean = sum(x*w) /sum(w),
    weighted_sd = sqrt(sum(w* x^2) / sum(w) - w_mean^2),
    category_counts = cat_counts
  )
}
# ---- Plot Function ----
plot.census <- function(x, num_var, cat_var) {
  if(nrow(x) > 5000){
    x <- x %>% slice_sample(n=5000)}
  
  ggplot(x, aes(x = .data[[cat_var]], 
                y = .data[[num_var]], 
                weight = PWGTP)) +
    geom_boxplot() + 
    labs(x = cat_var, y = num_var) + 
    theme_minimal()
}

title: ST_558_Project1
text:
Example

df <- query_census(
  num_var = c("AGEP","PWGTP"),
  cat_var = c("SEX", "SCHL"))

head(df)

# A tibble: 6 × 5
   AGEP PWGTP SEX    SCHL  state
  <dbl> <dbl> <fct>  <fct> <chr>

2    73    42 Male   16    12   
3    22    11 Male   19    26   
4    82    32 Female 23    17   
5    19    31 Female 16    36   
6    88    32 Male   22    12   

summary(df, num_var = "AGEP", cat_var = "SEX")

$weighted_mean
[1] 39.54931

$weighted_sd
[1] 23.26344

$category_counts

   Male  Female 
1656215 1717163 

plot(df, num_var = "AGEP", cat_var = "SEX")







# Multi-year example
df_multi <- query_census_multi(
  years = c(2019, 2021),
  num_var = c("AGEP", "PWGTP"),
  cat_var = "SEX",
  geography = "state",
  geo_filter = "37"
)

head(df_multi)
